title: Our next 20 years: AI, capitalism, and fractal minds
author: Judah Anttila
author_identity: Accelerated master's student in Industrial & Systems Engineering at OU, founder of Pristine Pens, co-founder of PhantomFit, founding president of OU Norman Next
description: It may cost less than $10,000 to replace a knowledge worker by 2030. Infinite life could be possible by 2040. This talk argues that these aren't science fiction — they follow directly from the exponential curve of computation, and the real question isn't whether they'll happen, but whether we'll be ready for them.
earlier_version: GSC Webinar, Feb 5, 2026 (58 views) — same talk given to Graduate Student Community at GCOE OU

key_arguments_from_analysis.1_knowledge_worker_collapse: By 2030, replacing a knowledge worker with AI agents will cost under $10,000. Companies will rent infinite agents instead of hiring. The Great Decoupling separates productivity from labor at scale for the first time.
key_arguments_from_analysis.2_longevity_escape_velocity: Radical longevity for wealthy by ~2032, broader access by 2040. Cites Kurzweil (AGI 2029 → aging becomes software problem) and Bryan Johnson Blueprint protocol as proof-of-concept.
key_arguments_from_analysis.3_fractal_mind_thesis: Consciousness is a pattern, not a substrate. Minds are recursive, self-similar information structures not locked to biological neurons. Can ingest into software, mathematics, wetware, or all three simultaneously.
key_arguments_from_analysis.4_emotional_register: Names three emotions: anxiety, anger, amazement — visible in every quarterly earnings call celebrating 'efficiency gains'.
key_arguments_from_analysis.5_rent_an_agent_economy: Already happening — companies moving entry-level analysts, junior coders, first-tier support to AI agent fleets costing pennies per task.

external_analysis.key_addition: Missing piece in Anttila's thesis is the substrate — wetware/biocomputing already filling that gap. Pure silicon AI runs hot and hungry; wetware outperforms by orders of magnitude on energy efficiency. Michael Levin's diverse intelligence research supports fractal mind thesis.

transcript_note: YouTube requires login for transcript; yt-dlp blocked by bot detection. Content reconstructed from description + biocomputer.com analysis + tavily search results.
